emotional_core: The cold clarity that arrives when you stop being confused about why authenticity is punished and recognize it as a system, not a mistake.
visual_metaphor: A cracked porcelain mask resting face-up on hard concrete — beautiful at distance, hollow underneath, the fracture revealing only more white emptiness
key_quotes: shitting all over the hearts of the good ones
Only to make themselves happy. Only to make themselves happy.
The waking up in the first line isn't despair. It's the end of being fooled.

story_frames.visual: Black screen, single white serif word appears center frame and holds for two seconds before fading
Slow pan across blurred crowd all facing one direction — one face in sharp focus, looking directly at camera, expression unreadable
Close-up of an ornate white mask laid face-up on cold stone floor, a single crack running through the center, harsh raking light from one side
A single open hand extended toward camera, palm up — a second hand enters frame and presses it down out of shot, slowly and deliberately
story_frames.text: Ready to tear down the true faces.

story_frames.visual: Empty mirror in a dimly lit room, reflection shows a standing figure — but no one visible in the actual frame, just the mirror
Same mirror, same room — the reflection is now gone too, just the empty glass and the dark room behind it
Person seated at a large window, back to camera, pale light coming through, completely still — no motion, no resolution, just presence
